            Multiplication is well-defined because for each fixed output pair (Оі, Оҙ), there are only
            finitely many decompositions
                                                             (Оі, Оҙ) = (Оұ, ОІ) + (ОұвҖІ , ОІ вҖІ ).

https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                                       381/481
29/04/2026, 23:52                                                                   Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            A linear map
                                                                T : Ocoord вҶ’ Ocoord     вҖӢ                             вҖӢ




            is coordinate-continuous if for every output monomial coefficient, the coefficient of
            T (F ) depends on only finitely many coefficients of F .

            This is the correct replacement for вҖңfinite windows.вҖқ
            A finite coordinate test is:
                                                              ПҖО“ : Ocoord вҶ’ вЁҒ C m
                                                                    вҖӢ           вҖӢ                             вҖӢ




                                                                                                 mвҲҲО“

            for a finite set О“ of monomials. Two coordinate-continuous maps are equal if and only if
            their composites with ПҖО“ agree for all finite О“.
                                                  вҖӢ




            3. Coordinate CE algebra of the formal disk
            Define
                                                              вҲҷ
                                                             CCE,coord (g) = вҲҸ C uОұ cОІ ,
                                                                            вҖӢ                             вҖӢ




                                                                                             Оұ,ОІ

            where
                                                                        g = h вӢү hвҲЁcont [1].           вҖӢ




            Here cI is dual to HI , and uI is dual to the shifted cotangent generator ОҙI [1].
                                            вҖӢ            вҖӢ                                                                вҖӢ




            Define the differential on generators by
                                                                       1
                                                             dCE cK = вҲ’ вҲ‘ CIJ c c .
                                                                           K I J
                                                                       2
                                                                вҖӢ                   вҖӢ             вҖӢ               вҖӢ




                                                                                            I,J

            For fixed K = (r, s), the condition
                                                                I + J вҲ’ (1, 1) = K

            has finitely many solutions in I Г— I . Thus dCEcK is a finite sum.               вҖӢ




            For uK , define
                       вҖӢ




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                    382/481
29/04/2026, 23:52                                                            Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                                             dCE uK = вҲ‘ CKJ
                                                                вҖӢ       вҖӢ
                                                                         L
                                                                            uL c J .
                                                                                  вҖӢ       вҖӢ           вҖӢ




                                                                            J,L

            For fixed K, J , L is determined by
                                                              L = K + J вҲ’ (1, 1).

            Hence dCEuK is generally infinite, but it is coordinate-continuous: for any fixed output
            monomial, only finitely many input monomials can contribute to its coefficient.
                               вҖӢ       вҖӢ




            Extend dCE as a graded derivation.
                                   вҖӢ




            Lemma 3.1: dCE is coordinate-continuous
                                            вҖӢ




            Fix an output monomial uОі cОҙ . In dCE(uОұ cОІ ), a contributing term arises by replacing one
            factor cK by two cвҖҷs or replacing one factor uK by uLcJ .
                                                                    вҖӢ




                                                                                      вҖӢ           вҖӢ




            Because uОі cОҙ has finite support, there are only finitely many positions and only finitely
            many possible K, L, J that can produce it. Therefore its coefficient in dF depends on
            finitely many coefficients of F .
            So dCE is a well-defined coordinate-continuous derivation.
                           вҖӢ




            Lemma 3.2: d2CE = 0                 вҖӢ




            It is enough to check on generators.
            For cK , d2cK = 0 is exactly the Jacobi identity for the structure constants CIJK .               вҖӢ




            For uK , the identity d2uK = 0 is exactly the representation identity for the coadjoint
            action.
                       вҖӢ                                вҖӢ




            Every coefficient of d2uK is a finite sum, so the formal coordinate calculation is
            legitimate.
                                                    вҖӢ




            Thus
                                                                 вҲҷ
                                                               (CCE,coord (g), dCE )
                                                                              вҖӢ               вҖӢ           вҖӢ




            is a well-defined coordinate dg commutative algebra.

            6. Bracket-admissible P0 -realizations                                вҖӢ




            The coordinate theorem is not yet a global P0-theorem. To get one, define admissibility.   вҖӢ

                                                       вҖӢ

            The Poisson bracket is continuous on Eq . Indeed, for monomials,                      вҖӢ

            9. Bar-cobar admissibility
            Do not use bar-cobar/Koszul enveloping duality for the full formal Hamiltonian algebra
            without hypotheses.
            A filtered Tate Lie algebra gadm is bar-cobar admissible if:
                                                              вҖӢ




             1. it has a complete separated descending filtration F вҲҷgadm ;
             2. [F p , F q ] вҠӮ F p+q ;
                                                                                                                            вҖӢ




             3. each grpF gadm is finite-dimensional;
             4. the CE coalgebra CвҲ—CE(gadm ) is conilpotent for the induced filtration;
                                 вҖӢ        вҖӢ




             5. the completed PBW filtration on U (gadm ) is complete and separated;
                                                      вҖӢ           вҖӢ




             6. the completed symmetric associated graded exists:
                                                                                            вҖӢ




                                                                      gr U (gadm ) вү… S (gadm ).
                                                                                        вҖӢ                               вҖӢ




            Only then assert
                                                                                                вҲј
                                                             О©CвҲ—CE (gadm )вҖӢ        вҖӢ                 вҖӢ   U (gadm ). вҖӢ




            The full Hamiltonian algebra h is perfect and has linear/quadratic nonconilpotent pieces.
            Pronilpotence must not be silently treated as conilpotence.

            The current draftвҖҷs theorem lane already states that the conilpotent bar-cobar template
            is used only in its own hypothesis, and that the untruncated formal Hamiltonian algebra
            is not used as lower-central conilpotent input.

            11. PBW ОЁ versus Matlis ПҒ
            The polynomial one-ПҲ descendants ОЁa,b are PBW special-fibre labels.вҖӢ




            They are not the Matlis coadjoint module.

                                        вҖӢ         вҖӢ                                   оҖ 0                                  вҖӢ




            for every r.

                                                                           вҹЁОЁa,b вҹ© вү…

            4. The reduction functor and why it loses information
            The reduced model is obtained from the unreduced open BV algebra by a composite of
            operations. Abstractly, write
                                                                                                                  pp
                                                             R : Obsopen вҹ¶ AвҲӮ .               вҖӢ                        вҖӢ




            This R is not a quasi-isomorphism of full P0-factorization algebras. It is a reduction
            procedure consisting of several non-faithful steps:
                                                                                      вҖӢ




                                            Obsopen вҹ¶ pointwise BV/Koszul model
                                                         вҖӢ




                                                                      вҹ¶ H вҲҷ (QKoszul )                    вҖӢ




                                                             вҹ¶ GLN -invariants    вҖӢ




                                      вҹ¶ N вҶ’ вҲһ degreewise stable connected traces

                                           вҹ¶ scalar-reduced Hamiltonian quotient

                                            вҹ¶ primitive indecomposable projection

                                                  вҹ¶ de Rham-current contraction

                                          вҹ¶ adjoined principal-part current model.

https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                             412/481
29/04/2026, 23:52                                                    Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            Each arrow loses structure.
            Most importantly:
                passing to H вҲҷ(Q) loses chain-level homotopies;
                passing to primitives kills decomposable products;
                scalar reduction kills the U (1) center;
                de Rham contraction kills positive interval-chain data;
                adjoining PI [1] introduces a reduced local-dual current sector not yet constructed
                inside Obsopen;
                                      вҖӢ




                stable large N erases finite-N trace identities;
                                          вҖӢ




                the principal-part currents are distributional, while the ordinary polynomial open
                observables do not realize them.
            The manuscript makes this distinction directly: the reduced principal-part current
            equation QОҳПҒ = 0 is part of an added reduced local-dual factor and is not yet derived
            from an unreduced de Rham current complex or Hochschild centrality homotopies.
                                  вҖӢ




                    main

            Therefore R is not conservative and not full on centers.

            5. Formal non-implication lemma
            Here is the elementary categorical reason the reduced theorem does not imply the
            unreduced theorem.
            Let
                                                               q : A вҹ¶ Ared       вҖӢ




            be a morphism of P0- or E1-algebras. It induces a map on centers
                                              вҖӢ          вҖӢ




                                                             Z(A) вҹ¶ Z(Ared ).         вҖӢ




            A central operation in Ared does not generally lift to a central operation in A.
                                                     вҖӢ




            Equivalently, a diagram
                                                              вҲҷ
                                                             CCEвҖӢ(g) вҹ¶ Z(Ared )           вҖӢ




            does not imply the existence of a lift

29/04/2026, 23:52                                                          Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                                                CCE (g) вҹ¶ Z(A).
                                                                      вҖӢ




            The obstruction is the homotopy fiber
                                                             Fib (Z(A) вҹ¶ Z(Ared )) .         вҖӢ




            More explicitly, a lift exists only if the reduced map lies in the image of
                                          вҲҷ                    вҲҷ
                                     Map(CCE (g), Z(A)) вҹ¶ Map(CCE
                                                      вҖӢ           (g), Z(Ared )).                вҖӢ        вҖӢ




            The reduced theorem only constructs the target point in the right-hand mapping space.
            It does not show that this point lifts.

            6. Elementary counterexample: abelianization does not lift
            centrality
            This is the simplest model of what goes wrong.
            Let
                                                                     A = CвҹЁx, yвҹ©

            be the free associative algebra, regarded as a locally constant factorization algebra on
            the line.
            Let
                                                             Ared = A/[x, y] вү… C[x, y].
                                                                 вҖӢ




            In Ared , the class of x is central.
                       вҖӢ




            So there is a degree-zero central element
                                                                     Лү вҲҲ Z(Ared ).
                                                                     x              вҖӢ




            But x вҲҲ A is not central, because
                                                                     xy вҲ’ yx =
                                                                             оҖ  0.

            Thus xЛү вҲҲ Z(Ared ) does not lift to an element of Z(A).
                                        вҖӢ




            In Hochschild language,

             centrality after quotient/reduction does not imply centrality before quotient/reduc

            The open BV model is subtler because

            9. Product centrality homotopies missing from the reduced
            model
            The full P0-factorization center is not just a Poisson bracket identity.
                            вҖӢ




            It contains product centrality.
            For a candidate central operation Z and an arbitrary unreduced open observable F , one
            needs a homotopy
                                                                            prod
                                                                    HZ,F            вҖӢ




            such that
                                                                                        prod         prod              prod
                    m(Z, F ) вҲ’ (вҲ’1)вҲЈZвҲЈвҲЈF вҲЈ m(F , Z) = Qopen HZ,F + HZ,QF + (вҲ’1)вҲЈF вҲЈ HQZ,F .    вҖӢ               вҖӢ              вҖӢ




            This equation must hold for all unreduced open observables F , not just for stable
            connected Hamiltonian traces.
            The reduced model checks product commutativity only after:
             1. passing to stable connected traces;
             2. killing decomposables;

            inside the unreduced open BV algebra.

            11. The principal-part current sector is adjoined in the

            The reduced principal-part model introduces generators

            In the reduced model,

            because ОҳПҒ is placed in an added local-dual factor.

                                                                                     вҖӢ

                                                                                                             вҖӢ




            complex, a chain-level primitive projection, or Hochschild centrality homotopies.

            That is a different theorem.

            12. The primitive projection is only an indecomposable
            shadow
            The chain-level primitive projection kills decomposable products. But the factorization
            center must control products.
            The manuscript explicitly says the primitive projection gives only the primitive
            indecomposable P0-shadow of the reduced comparison, and that it does not construct
            the unreduced cotangent factorization-center morphism or its centrality homotopies.
                                           вҖӢ




                    main

            This is a fundamental logical point.
            A factorization-center theorem must know how an operation acts on:
                                                                  F1 F2 ,
                                                                        вҖӢ         вҖӢ




                                                                 F1 F2 F3 ,
                                                                    вҖӢ         вҖӢ           вҖӢ




            and all higher products in the open algebra.
            An indecomposable projection
                                                                  ПҖprim               вҖӢ




            is designed to remove such products.
            Therefore a theorem after ПҖprim cannot, by itself, prove a theorem about the full
            factorization center.
                                                             вҖӢ




            It proves only the primitive shadow.

            13. Polynomial one-ПҲ descendants cannot realize principal
            parts
            The full unreduced theorem needs boundary representatives for the shifted cotangent
            generators.
            A tempting idea is to use the polynomial one-ПҲ classes
https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                 420/481
29/04/2026, 23:52                                                                             Whitepaper Critical Analysis

                                                                                      ОЁa,b .     вҖӢ




            But this is ruled out.
            In the polynomial descendant sector,
                                                              O1,0 : ОЁa,b вҶҰ b ОЁa,bвҲ’1 .
                                                                          вҖӢ               вҖӢ                      вҖӢ




            In the principal-part module,
                                                         z1 : ПҒa,b вҶҰ вҲ’(b + 1)ПҒa,b+1 .
                                                              вҖӢ               вҖӢ                                              вҖӢ




            Similarly,
                                                             O0,1 : ОЁa,b вҶҰ вҲ’a ОЁaвҲ’1,b ,
                                                                      вҖӢ               вҖӢ                              вҖӢ




            whereas
                                                             z2 : ПҒa,b вҶҰ (a + 1)ПҒa+1,b .
                                                                  вҖӢ               вҖӢ                                      вҖӢ




            So the polynomial one-ПҲ action and the principal-part coadjoint action shift in opposite
            directions. The manuscript records this explicitly and concludes that the primitive
            projection records labels only; it does not supply centrality homotopies against arbitrary
            unreduced open observables. main
            More invariantly:
                 linear Hamiltonians act locally nilpotently on polynomial observables;
                 linear Hamiltonians do not act locally nilpotently on principal parts.
            Therefore no ordinary polynomial boundary observable sector can realize the principal-
            part coadjoint module.
            Hence the reduced principal-part current target is not obtained from the existing
            polynomial open BV algebra by the primitive projection.
            It has to be built as an additional distributional boundary sector with its own centrality
            homotopies.

            15. Factorization variance is another missing ingredient
            There is also a variance issue.
            The interval Hamiltonian current algebra
                                                                     hI = О©вҲҷc (I)вҠ—h
                                                                       вҖӢ         вҖӢ                вҖӢ




            is covariant under extension by zero:
                                                                 IвҠӮJ       вҮ’             hI вҶ’ hJ .
                                                                                              вҖӢ           вҖӢ




            Thus it is a cosheaf.

https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                                422/481
29/04/2026, 23:52                                                              Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            CE chains are covariant with this cosheaf. CE cochains are contravariant unless one
            passes to a compactly supported density-dual model.
            A full factorization theorem must therefore specify whether it uses:
             1. CE chains and dualizes later;
             2. compactly supported density-dual observables;
             3. a finite/reflexive admissible envelope;
             4. a cofactorization formulation.
            The reduced current model can hide this variance because it works with explicit
            smeared currents and strict brackets.
            The full theorem cannot hide it.
            It must give the coproduct-to-product equations for factorization products on disjoint
            intervals.
            For disjoint intervals
                                                                 I1 , вҖҰ , In вҠӮ J,
                                                                   вҖӢ               вҖӢ




            one needs maps
                                      Obsopen (I1 ) вҠ— вӢҜ вҠ— Obsopen (In ) вҹ¶ Obsopen (J)
                                                    вҖӢ    вҖӢ                                 вҖӢ   вҖӢ              вҖӢ




            and corresponding centrality homotopies natural with respect to those maps.
            The reduced pointwise bracket
                                                        {Bf (a), Bg (b)} = B{f ,g} (ab)
                                                             вҖӢ             вҖӢ                       вҖӢ




            does not supply these higher factorization coherence equations.

            16. The scalar U (1) obstruction is killed in the reduced
            model
            The full unreduced theorem must also deal with the scalar line.
            Before scalar reduction,
                                                             {Tr П•1 , Tr П•2 } = N .
                                                                       вҖӢ               вҖӢ




https://chatgpt.com/c/69efa84c-a760-83ea-ae70-183a06ecd2d3                                                        423/481
29/04/2026, 23:52                                                              Whitepaper Critical Analysis

            After scalar reduction this is killed.
            On the closed side, the scalar cocycle is
                                                                    Пү(f , g) = [{f , g}]0 .     вҖӢ




            The scalar-reduced Hamiltonian algebra has no constant Hamiltonian line on which the
            primitive
                                                                       О·(f ) = вҲ’[f ]0      вҖӢ




            can live.
            Quantum mechanically, the same scalar appears as the Capelli/Weyl shift
                             Tr(AXY ) вҲ’ Tr(AY X) = в„ҸN Tr(A) mod moment ideal.

            With the central ghost Оі1, the scalar contact representative is
                                                   вҖӢ

                                                                вҖӢ




                                                                           в„ҸN [cЛү].

            Alternatively, one changes the data by using a central character, an unreduced scalar
            primitive, or a balanced supertrace. The manuscript states these alternatives and treats
            the scalar QME obstruction as a real obstruction class. main
            The reduced Hamiltonian current model avoids this obstruction by quotienting the

            Therefore it cannot prove the unreduced scalar-compatible theorem.

            17. The analytic Costello/QME data are also absent

            21. Why the reduced theorem is still valuable
            The reduced theorem is not wrong or weak. It proves exactly the correct shadow:
                                                               вҲҷ

                                                                   вҖӢ




            and the reduced brane current realization

                                                             cI вҶҰ Оё I ,         uI вҶҰ O I ,
                                                                                      вҖӢ        вҖӢ




            and the correct reduced principal-part brackets.
            This is the mathematical core of the closed-open picture.
            But it is a theorem about the reduced Hamiltonian current model, not a theorem about
            the full unreduced open BV factorization center.
            The manuscriptвҖҷs current status ledger says exactly this: the reduced coordinate CE/PV
            comparison is proved under the stated continuity conditions, while the full Zfact
                                                                                         P

            open BV algebra remains open and requires centrality homotopies, descent, and QME
                                                                                                                 вҖӢ




            data. main

            Proof
            The reduction R is not an equivalence of full P0-factorization algebras. It passes
            through cohomology, stable connected trace extraction, scalar reduction, primitive
                                                                                         вҖӢ




            indecomposable projection, de Rham contraction, and an adjoined local-dual current
            sector. These operations are not conservative and do not preserve or reflect full derived
            centers.
            An elementary counterexample is the quotient
                                                                      CвҹЁx, yвҹ© вҶ’ C[x, y].

            The image of x is central in the quotient but x is not central in the free algebra. Hence
            centrality in a reduced quotient does not imply centrality before quotient.
            In the present model, the reduced current theorem proves strict identities only in
                                                             pp
                                                         AвҲӮ (I) = S (hI )вҠ—S (PI [1]).
                                                                  вҖӢ              вҖӢ           вҖӢ   вҖӢ




            The unreduced theorem requires chain-level centrality homotopies against arbitrary
            observables in
                                                                         Obsopen (I).вҖӢ




            The primitive projection records only the indecomposable shadow and does not control
            products. The principal-part current ОҳПҒ is an added reduced local-dual generator and is
            not yet derived from an unreduced boundary BV current complex. The polynomial
                                                                           вҖӢ




            one-ПҲ classes cannot supply it because their Hamiltonian action does not match the
            Matlis coadjoint action. Finally, the scalar U (1) class is killed by the reduced quotient
            but must be cancelled or absorbed in an unreduced theory.
            Therefore the reduced Hamiltonian current model does not prove the full unreduced P0-
            factorization-center theorem. в–Ў
                                                                                                              вҖӢ

            9. Factorization compatibility
            For inclusions j : I вҶӘ J , the map
                                                                                                 j! : pI вҶ’ pJ
                                                                                                     вҖӢ               вҖӢ                   вҖӢ




            induces
                                                      j! : Obsopen (I) вҶ’ Obsopen (J).
                                                         вҖӢ                                               вҖӢ                                           вҖӢ




            It also induces a map of shifted cotangent Lie algebras
                                                                                                         gI вҶ’ gJ
                                                                                                             вҖӢ                   вҖӢ




            after applying extension by zero to pI and pushforward to the compactly supported
            dual.
                                                                                                 вҖӢ




            The CE/PV map is natural in dg Lie maps. Therefore the diagram
                                                    вҲҷ
                                                   CCE (gI ) вҹ¶ PV(Obsopen (I))
                                                                 вҖӢ                   вҖӢ                                                                       вҖӢ




                                                      вҶ“             вҶ“                                            вҖӢ                                                           вҖӢ




                                                    вҲҷ
                                                   CCE (gJ ) вҹ¶ PV(Obsopen (J))
                                                             вҖӢ                           вҖӢ                                                               вҖӢ




            commutes.
            For disjoint intervals I1, вҖҰ , In вҠӮ J , the factorization product is multiplication after
            extension by zero. The CE/PV map is multiplicative, so it is compatible with the disjoint-
                                               вҖӢ                     вҖӢ




            interval products.
            Thus the intervalwise isomorphisms assemble into a morphism of locally constant P0-
            factorization algebras:
                                                                                                                                                                                 вҖӢ




                                                       вҲҷ             P0
                                                      CCE (g(вҲ’) ) вҹ¶ Zfact (Obsopen ).
                                                                             вҖӢ                   вҖӢ
                                                                                                                             вҖӢ




                                                                                                                                     вҖӢ                           вҖӢ       вҖӢ




            This proves the full factorization theorem in the stable primitive cyclic open BV model.

            10. The Hamiltonian sector as a quotient of the full theorem
            Now recover the Hamiltonian BF theorem.
            Let

            if w contains no ПҲ, and

            if w contains at least one ПҲ.
            This is a chain map because if w = ПҲv, then

            and both terms have the same commutative monomial image, hence cancel under N .
            It is also a Lie map to the commutative Hamiltonian algebra:

            11. The normal-ordering homotopy

            16. Factorization coherence of the homotopies
            For intervals I вҠӮ J , extension by zero commutes with:
                                              N,             i,               H,                             Q,                   {вҲ’, вҲ’}open .  вҖӢ




            Indeed:
                N acts only on cyclic words, not on the interval form;
                i acts only on cyclic words;
                H bubble-sorts only the word part;
                Q is ddR + Qcyc , both compatible with extension by zero;
                the bracket is the wedge product in О©вҲҷc (I) times the necklace bracket.
                                вҖӢ             вҖӢ




                                                                                               вҖӢ




            Thus
                                                                              j ! HI = HJ j !
                                                                               вҖӢ       вҖӢ                          вҖӢ       вҖӢ




            for every inclusion j : I вҶӘ J .
            For disjoint intervals I1, вҖҰ , In вҠӮ J , the product is multiplication after extension by
            zero. Since H is a derivation homotopy on the free symmetric algebra extension, the
                                                  вҖӢ           вҖӢ




            homotopies satisfy the usual Leibniz formula:
                                                                  n
                                H(F1 вӢҜ Fn ) = вҲ‘(вҲ’1)вҲЈF1 вҲЈ+вӢҜ+вҲЈFrвҲ’1 вҲЈ F1 вӢҜ H(Fr ) вӢҜ Fn .
                                          вҖӢ           вҖӢ               вҖӢ
                                                                                           вҖӢ                                  вҖӢ




                                                                                                                                   вҖӢ        вҖӢ       вҖӢ




                                                              r=1

            Therefore the product and bracket centrality homotopies are compatible with disjoint
            factorization products.
            This gives the factorization-level theorem, not merely a stalkwise theorem.

            17. Principal parts inside the full theorem
            The full primitive theorem uses
                                                                                         pвҲЁopen .        вҖӢ




            The Hamiltonian BF sector uses the quotient
                                                                         Nred : popen вҶ’ h.
                                                                                 вҖӢ                           вҖӢ




            Its continuous dual is the injection
                                                                             hвҲЁcont вҶӘ pвҲЁopen .
                                                                                         вҖӢ                               вҖӢ




            The Matlis theorem identifies
                                                                 hвҲЁcont вү… P = вЁҒ CПҒa,b ,
                                                                         вҖӢ                                           вҖӢ                   вҖӢ




                                                                                                      a+b>0

            where
                                                                 ПҒa,b = z1вҲ’aвҲ’1 z2вҲ’bвҲ’1 dz1 dz2 .
                                                                     вҖӢ                           вҖӢ               вҖӢ               вҖӢ           вҖӢ




            The residue pairing is
                                                                  вҹЁПҒa,b , z1m z2n вҹ© = Оҙa,m Оҙb,n .
                                                                             вҖӢ       вҖӢ       вҖӢ                               вҖӢ       вҖӢ




            The coadjoint action is
                                           p q
                                         z1 z2 вӢ… ПҒa,b = вҲ’(pb вҲ’ qa + p вҲ’ q)ПҒaвҲ’p+1,bвҲ’q+1 .

            VI. The Hamiltonian sector inside the full theorem

            VII. The bubble-sort contraction
            Define a section
                                                                             i : hpoly вҶ’ pH            вҖӢ                               вҖӢ




            by
                                                                         i(z1a z2b ) = [xa y b ].

            For any word w = w(x, y), let
                                                            a = #x(w),                                         b = #y(w).

            Choose the deterministic bubble-sort path

            where each step replaces a subword yx by xy.

            Therefore

            on pH .  вҖӢ
